seoTitle: Delete a Journal Entry in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Find and safely remove a mistaken or duplicate journal entry in QuickBooks Online, and confirm the removal in the Audit Log.
intro: A double-posted or incorrect journal entry can throw off your general ledger if left uncorrected. This guide shows how to locate, review, and delete a journal entry in QuickBooks Online, plus how to verify the change afterward.

steps.heading: Locate the entry
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then Chart of Accounts, open the relevant register, or use Search and filter by Journal Entries.

steps.heading: Open the transaction
steps.body: Click the journal entry row to expand the details, then click Edit to open the original form.

steps.heading: Review the debit and credit lines
steps.body: Audit all balancing lines to confirm this is the entry you actually want to remove.

steps.heading: Execute the deletion
steps.body: Open the More options drawer at the bottom and select Delete.

steps.heading: Confirm and verify
steps.body: Click Yes on the confirmation prompt, then check the Audit Log or your general ledger to confirm the row is gone and balances recalculated.

faq.question: Should I delete or void a journal entry?
faq.answer: Deleting removes it entirely, while voiding keeps a record with zeroed values; voiding is often preferred for entries tied to closed periods.

faq.question: How do I find a specific journal entry to delete?
faq.answer: Use the Search magnifying glass in the top right and filter by Journal Entries, or browse your Chart of Accounts register.

faq.question: Will deleting a journal entry affect my trial balance?
faq.answer: Yes, since it removes the debit and credit lines, your account balances will recalculate immediately.

faq.question: Can I see who deleted a journal entry?
faq.answer: Yes, the Audit Log tracks deletions along with the user who made the change.
